title: 2x2 DIY Wood Pumpkin Decor
summary: Three short 2x2 blocks glued face to face into a squat pumpkin shape — a five-minute project once the pieces are cut.
description:
About as minimal a build as this catalog has: three lengths of 2x2 glued together side by side, topped with a small stem piece (or a real branch, if one's handy). There's no fastening required at all beyond glue, though a few brad nails add insurance while the glue cures if you're impatient to move it.

The only real decision is proportions — three 6" pieces make a shorter, squatter pumpkin, while three 4" pieces make a taller, narrower one. Both use the same three-block construction, just different piece lengths.

tools.note: Only needed if trimming stock from a longer board.

materials.name: Wood glue
materials.note: Hot glue works well too — it sets fast and strong.

materials.name: Brad nails, 2"
materials.note: Optional, for extra hold while the glue cures.

steps.title: Glue the blocks together
steps.body: Glue the three 2x2 pieces together side by side and let dry fully before handling. Hot glue is preferred since it sets fast and strong, but any wood glue works — just give it time to cure. A few 2" brad nails add extra hold if you have a nailer on hand.

steps.title: Finish and attach the stem
steps.body: Paint or stain the glued-up blocks and the stem piece, then attach the stem with glue or a brad nail.

images.alt: Small 2x2 wood pumpkin decor for a mantle or tabletop
